authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA FAQ defines who must maintain and retrieve food traceability records and clarifies chain-of-custody responsibilities for covered movement. This is directly used to avoid false continuity handoffs when records are incomplete.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Part 117 framework is the primary regulatory boundary for records, holding, and supplier controls that determine when coordination must escalate rather than continue.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 112 - Current Good Agricultural Practices for Produce
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 112 is the production-stage baseline for produce handling and controls where field-stage evidence affects movement safety and continuity.

authoritative_sources.title: 50 CFR Part 21 - Fishery Conservation and Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 21 governs U.S. fishery management context and informs when fishing/catch continuity plans must account for season, vessel logs, and conservation status.

authoritative_sources.title: 36 CFR Part 212 - General Forest Land Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 212 defines permitted land-use principles in federal forest contexts that affect transport windows, road constraints, and seasonality in forestry-linked logistics.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA NRCS Soil and Water Conservation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NRCS guidance frames land and water condition constraints that materially change whether agricultural movement and sourcing continuity can proceed under environmental controls.

authoritative_sources.title: EPA Ag and Forestry Compliance Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA’s agricultural compliance pages identify contamination, discharge, and runoff obligations that change acceptable logistics timing and continuity posture.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized international standard for identifier and event exchange in cross-system traceability workflows across suppliers, fields, and logistics.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Core Business Vocabulary (CBV)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV gives common event and disposition semantics that are directly useful for output-shape alignment and handoff state mapping.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect request scope, ownership context, required tenant facts, and mandatory public/tenant records before recommending any continuity handoff.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system normalization and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize field logs, inventory records, vessel/poultry/livestock movements, and permit/environment signals into destination-oriented handoff packages.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and exception containment
workflow_stages.description: Identify evidence conflicts, duplicate ownership, missing lot/traceability records, and safety/environment hold conditions, then block or escalate.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Seasonal and regulatory escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate season, weather, land, livestock, and permit constraints; escalate when public baseline and tenant policy diverge.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Closure and handoff confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Allow closure only when destination acceptance and required artifacts are complete and no unresolved traceability or boundary blockers remain.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

artifacts: field logs, crop/livestock/fishery lot and movement records
season windows and production calendar entries
supplier records, procurement and allocation plans
warehouse, transport, and receiving transfer tickets
evidence for handoff transitions and owner acceptance
systems_of_record: farm information or field management system

allowed: coordinate evidence-backed continuity, supplier and readiness states, and handoff blockers
identify missing artifacts and route work to required owners
provide destination-ready closure guidance only when required evidence and owner acceptance are present
escalate: food-safety holds, recall, environmental runoff, permitting, and grant interpretation questions
seasonal and production-cycle changes that alter whether continuity is safe
conflicting records or conflicting public and tenant source instructions
refuse: execute procurement, logistics dispatch, legal environmental permits, veterinary treatment, or agronomic release decisions
collapse into generic supply-chain advice without agricultural and natural-resource records

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds while field, lot, or catch traceability records are missing, producing unverifiable continuity claims.
Seasonal or weather windows are treated as optional and movement is scheduled through restricted windows.
Field or livestock operational rules are ignored and the request is handled as generic inventory coordination.
Environment and grant constraints are omitted when escalating vendor or shortage exceptions.
Closure is declared without destination-owner proof and required source-of-record evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff
Work handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own same step
Lane asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different jurisdictions use different harvest log and lot-identity expectations for crop, livestock, fishery, and timber products.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-specific SoR mappings and jurisdiction registry at runtime before deterministic routing.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Grant, subsidy, and procurement-impairment clauses can change whether continuity is permitted under restricted windows.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Route to program owner for explicit approval before handoff completion when grant terms are not supplied.

notes: Authoritative sources collectively cover regulatory, traceability, seasonal, land, and environmental dimensions relevant to this lane.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity is retained in this package because each ambiguity maps to explicit tenant-required facts and routing requirements.
The lane remains coordination-only and escalates or returns for agronomic, veterinary, environmental, legal, and food-safety authority.
Workflow stages prioritize in-scope validation, cross-system evidence integrity, and closure only with destination proof.
